sections.paragraph: only in Pilpel צִפְצַף an onomatop. word, to twitter, pip, or chirp, as a bird, Isa 10:14 38:14 , Gr. πιπίζω, τιτίζω, Germ. zirpen. Like the Greek τρίζω, στρουθίζω, it is applied to the slender voice of the manes, “vocem exiguam” (Virg. Æn. vi. 492), which the ancients sometimes compared to a whisper, sometimes to a sigh, Isa 8:19. [But why should we look in the word of God for such heathen ideas as Manes?]
